learning_concepts.title: Speed Control
learning_concepts.summary: Control turn order with Tailwind, Trick Room, paralysis, priority or speed drops.
learning_concepts.meaning: Speed control helps your team move before the opponent through effects like Tailwind, Trick Room, Icy Wind or paralysis.
learning_concepts.matters: Moving first often decides games. Fast teams keep momentum, while slower teams need ways to compensate.
mistakes: Mixing fast and slow Pokémon poorly
Relying on only one speed option
learning_concepts.checks: The builder checks average team speed, priority access, Tailwind users, Trick Room setters and speed-lowering support.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: Weather Teams
learning_concepts.summary: Use Rain, Sun, Sand or Snow to power up specific Pokémon and strategies.
learning_concepts.meaning: Weather teams are built around Rain, Sun, Sand or Snow to boost specific Pokémon and moves.
learning_concepts.matters: Weather can increase damage, activate abilities and shape entire matchups.
supports: Weather abusers like Swift Swim or Chlorophyll
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: Trick Room
learning_concepts.summary: Reverse turn order so slow, bulky attackers can move first.
learning_concepts.meaning: Trick Room reverses move order so slower Pokémon act first.
learning_concepts.matters: It allows bulky, powerful Pokémon to overwhelm faster offensive teams.
mistakes: Using Pokémon that are too fast
No protection for Trick Room setup
Weak backup mode outside Trick Room
learning_concepts.checks: The builder checks team speed balance, Trick Room setters, slow attackers and support options that help setup safely.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: Balance Teams
learning_concepts.matters: Balanced teams are flexible and can adapt during longer games.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: Hyper Offense
learning_concepts.summary: Stack fast momentum and aggressive momentum to overwhelm opponents quickly.
learning_concepts.meaning: Hyper offense focuses on constant momentum and aggressive momentum.
learning_concepts.matters: These teams try to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize.
learning_concepts.checks: The builder checks offensive momentum, speed tiers, setup options and how quickly the team threatens knockouts.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: Redirection
learning_concepts.summary: Protect key teammates with moves that draw attacks away from them.
learning_concepts.meaning: Redirection forces attacks toward a support Pokémon using moves like Follow Me or Rage Powder.
learning_concepts.matters: It protects teammates while they setup or attack safely.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: covering multiple team jobs
learning_concepts.summary: Use Pokémon that cover several jobs without wasting team slots.
learning_concepts.meaning: covering multiple team jobs means one Pokémon performs multiple useful jobs for the team.
learning_concepts.matters: It saves team slots and improves flexibility.
learning_concepts.checks: The builder checks how many roles each Pokémon fills and whether important roles overlap too heavily.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: Win Conditions
learning_concepts.summary: Understand the main route your team uses to actually close games.
learning_concepts.meaning: A win condition is the main strategy your team uses to secure victories.
learning_concepts.matters: Without a clear win condition, teams often lack direction.
learning_concepts.checks: The builder identifies primary endgame win condition setup pieces, setup options, endgame momentum and overall team direction.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.

learning_concepts.title: Lead Selection
learning_concepts.summary: Choose opening pairs that create momentum from turn one.
learning_concepts.meaning: Leads are the Pokémon you send out first to establish momentum or control.
learning_concepts.matters: Strong leads shape the pace of the match immediately.
learning_concepts.confidenceReason: Strategic/analysis metadata preserved as experimental; not official Pokémon Champions battle data.
